data.title: Polymarket traders bet $2M on Ghislaine Maxwell outcomes after secret DOJ meeting and congressional subpoena.
data.description: Activity on the cryptocurrency-powered prediction platform Polymarket has experienced a significant increase following an unexpected development involving Ghislaine Maxwell. The former associate of Jeffrey Epstein had a meeting with U.S. Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche on July 24, leading to a surge in speculation and bets on various Epstein-related scenarios. Maxwell's lawyer, David Oscar Markus, stated that the meeting was "very productive," highlighting her full cooperation and lack of legal privilege assertions during questioning. Subsequently, a House committee issued a subpoena on the same day, compelling Maxwell to testify publicly on August 11, which indicates a notable shift in the public legal actions concerning the Epstein case. Consequently, traders on Polymarket are actively betting on outcomes related to the Epstein case. One of the most popular markets on the platform is "Will Ghislaine Maxwell testify before Congress in 2025?" with a high level of activity.

data.title: Analyst warns of Apple's unclear AI strategy impacting stock, raising concerns. Wall Street cautioned investors.
data.description: An analyst from Wall Street has issued a cautionary statement regarding Apple's stock, citing the company's ambiguous approach towards artificial intelligence. The lack of a well-defined strategy in this area has raised concerns, prompting the analyst to label it as a warning sign for investors. The article titled "Apple's Lack Of Clear AI Strategy A Warning Flag, Analyst Says" was featured on Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Joseph Chalom of BlackRock to co-lead SharpLink Gaming, world's 2nd-largest Ethereum holder ($1.34B), for DeFi innovation.
data.description: Joseph Chalom, former head of digital assets strategy at BlackRock, has been named co-CEO of SharpLink Gaming, Inc., which holds 360,807 ETH valued at approximately $1.34 billion as of July 20, 2025. This move highlights SharpLink's focus on leading in Ethereum-based DeFi and digital asset innovation. Chalom will work alongside current CEO Rob Phythian, who will transition to president while staying on the company's board. SharpLink is known for advocating Ethereum adoption and plans to bridge traditional finance with blockchain technology. Chalom's experience in launching BlackRock's iShares Ethereum Trust, with over $10 billion in assets, positions him to drive SharpLink's vision. Joseph Lubin, SharpLink's chairman and Ethereum co-founder, sees Chalom's appointment as validation of the company's ETH treasury strategy. Chalom expressed his excitement for SharpLink's commitment to the Ethereum ecosystem and innovative scaling.

data.title: Experts believe AI can enhance crypto security, as smart-contract developers see improvements in code quality and safety.
data.description: Although previous studies raised concerns, smart contract developers and auditors have reported to Cointelegraph that the use of AI technology in coding is enhancing the security of cryptocurrencies.
